summary: Educational assistance for spouses and dependents of disabled veterans in Ohio.
eligibility: Spouses and dependents of veterans with 100% service-connected disability.

benefit_name: Ohio Veteran Survivor and Dependent Benefits
summary: Ohio offers state-level benefits for surviving spouses, dependents, and family members of veterans. Programs may include education tuition waivers or scholarships, survivor pension supplements, priority access to state veteran services, and continued eligibility for select state programs. Specific programs and eligibility requirements vary.
eligibility: Surviving spouses, unremarried widows/widowers, and dependent children of eligible Ohio veterans. Some programs require the veteran to have had a service-connected disability or to have died in service. Residency in Ohio is typically required. Contact the Ohio veterans agency for current program availability.

benefit_name: Ohio Enhanced Benefits for Service-Connected Disabled Veterans
summary: Ohio provides a package of enhanced state benefits for veterans with a service-connected disability rating. Benefits may include increased property tax exemptions at higher rating tiers, priority access to state veteran services, healthcare facility preference, adaptive equipment assistance, and waived fees across multiple state programs. Specific benefits and thresholds vary by rating level.
eligibility: Veterans with any service-connected disability rating who are residents of Ohio. Many programs require a minimum 10% rating; higher-value benefits typically require 50% or 100% ratings. Contact the Ohio veterans agency for current eligibility thresholds.

summary: Property tax exemption for veterans with 100% total service-connected disability in Ohio. The enhanced homestead exemption provides approximately $56,000 in property value reduction for qualifying disabled veterans.
eligibility: Veterans with 100% total service-connected disability rating, residing in Ohio as primary homestead.

benefit_name: OHFA Next Home Program – Veteran Housing Assistance
summary: The Ohio Housing Finance Agency (OHFA) Next Home Program provides eligible veterans with 30-year fixed-rate mortgage loans at competitive interest rates, along with optional down payment assistance of 2.5%–5% of the purchase price. Unlike most programs, Next Home does not require first-time homebuyer status for veterans. Down payment assistance is forgiven after a minimum holding period.
eligibility: Honorably discharged Ohio veterans. Income and purchase price limits apply by county. Loans must be originated through an OHFA-approved lender. Visit myohiohome.org or call 888-362-6432 for current income limits and participating lenders.

summary: Discounted or free hunting and fishing licenses for disabled veterans in Ohio.
eligibility: Disabled veterans with service-connected disabilities.

summary: In-state tuition rates for veterans at Ohio public universities and colleges.
eligibility: Honorably discharged veterans who are state residents.

summary: Exemption or exclusion of military retirement income from Ohio state income tax.
eligibility: Veterans receiving military retirement pay.

summary: State-operated long-term care facility for eligible Ohio veterans.
eligibility: Ohio resident veterans with honorable discharge.

summary: Fee waivers for professional license applications for Ohio veterans.
eligibility: Honorably discharged veterans.

summary: Business certification and support for service-disabled veteran entrepreneurs in Ohio.
eligibility: Veterans with 10% or higher service-connected disability rating.

summary: Hiring preference for state government positions for Ohio veterans.
eligibility: Honorably discharged veterans.

summary: Cemetery providing burial services and perpetual care for Ohio veterans.
eligibility: Honorably discharged veterans and eligible family members.

summary: Special veteran identification license plates for Ohio veterans.
eligibility: Honorably discharged veterans.

benefit_name: PHFA Keystone Home Loan Program for Veterans
summary: The Pennsylvania Housing Finance Agency (PHFA) Keystone Home Loan and K-Gov programs provide veterans with 30-year fixed-rate mortgages and flexible eligibility. The K-Gov option has no income or purchase price limits and accepts FHA, USDA, and VA loans. Additional closing cost and down payment assistance up to $6,000 may be available through PHFA secondary mortgage programs.
eligibility: Honorably discharged Pennsylvania veteran residents. No first-time homebuyer requirement. K-Gov program has no income limits. Applications must be made through a PHFA-approved lender. Call 1-855-827-3466 or visit phfa.org for a lender directory.

summary: Property tax exemption for disabled veterans with service-connected disabilities in Texas.
eligibility: Veterans with 10% or higher service-connected disability rating.

benefit_name: Veterans Land Board Home Loan Program
summary: The Texas Veterans Land Board (VLB) offers three loan programs: home loans at below-market interest rates, land loans for purchase of Texas land, and home improvement loans. Texas resident veterans can access these state-originated loan products directly through the VLB.
eligibility: Texas resident veterans with honorable discharge and minimum service requirements. Surviving spouses of eligible veterans may also qualify.

summary: Discounted or free hunting and fishing licenses for disabled veterans in Texas.
eligibility: Disabled veterans with service-connected disabilities.

summary: In-state tuition rates for veterans at Texas public universities and colleges.
eligibility: Honorably discharged veterans who are state residents.

benefit_name: Texas Professional License Fee Waivers for Veterans
summary: Texas waives initial application and licensing fees for honorably discharged veterans across dozens of regulated professions. Administered by individual licensing boards including TDLR (HVAC, electrician, cosmetology, auctioneers), the Texas Medical Board, Texas Board of Nursing, and others. Veterans should contact the board for their specific profession to confirm eligibility and apply.
eligibility: Honorably discharged veterans applying for an initial Texas professional license. The fee waiver typically applies to first-time license applications only, not renewals. Specific professions and waiver amounts vary by licensing board.
administering_agency: Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation (TDLR) and other TX licensing boards

summary: Business certification and support for service-disabled veteran entrepreneurs in Texas.
eligibility: Veterans with 10% or higher service-connected disability rating.

summary: Hiring preference for state government positions for Texas veterans.
eligibility: Honorably discharged veterans.

summary: Cemetery providing burial services and perpetual care for Texas veterans.
eligibility: Honorably discharged veterans and eligible family members.

summary: The Texas Veterans Land Board (VLB) operates a network of state veterans homes offering skilled nursing, assisted living, and memory care for eligible Texas veterans. Facilities located in Amarillo, Big Spring, Bonham, El Paso, Floresville, McAllen, Temple, and Tyler. Priority admission for veterans with service-connected conditions.
eligibility: Texas resident veterans with honorable discharge. Admission is prioritized by level of need and service connection. Spouses and Gold Star parents may also be eligible. Contact the VLB for current availability and admission criteria.

summary: Texas offers a wide range of specialty license plates for veterans and active duty service members, including branch-specific plates (Army, Navy, Air Force, Marines, Coast Guard, Space Force), Combat Veteran, Medal of Honor, POW/MIA, Purple Heart, and Disabled Veteran plates. Many veteran plates carry reduced or waived fees for qualifying veterans.
eligibility: Honorably discharged veterans. Specific plates (Combat Veteran, Purple Heart, Disabled Veteran) require documentation of qualifying service or award. Applications through Texas DMV or county tax-assessor collector offices.

summary: Educational assistance for spouses and dependents of disabled veterans in Utah.
eligibility: Spouses and dependents of veterans with 100% service-connected disability.

benefit_name: Vermont Veteran Survivor and Dependent Benefits
summary: Vermont offers state-level benefits for surviving spouses, dependents, and family members of veterans. Programs may include education tuition waivers or scholarships, survivor pension supplements, priority access to state veteran services, and continued eligibility for select state programs. Specific programs and eligibility requirements vary.
eligibility: Surviving spouses, unremarried widows/widowers, and dependent children of eligible Vermont veterans. Some programs require the veteran to have had a service-connected disability or to have died in service. Residency in Vermont is typically required. Contact the Vermont veterans agency for current program availability.

benefit_name: Vermont Enhanced Benefits for Service-Connected Disabled Veterans
summary: Vermont provides a package of enhanced state benefits for veterans with a service-connected disability rating. Benefits may include increased property tax exemptions at higher rating tiers, priority access to state veteran services, healthcare facility preference, adaptive equipment assistance, and waived fees across multiple state programs. Specific benefits and thresholds vary by rating level.
eligibility: Veterans with any service-connected disability rating who are residents of Vermont. Many programs require a minimum 10% rating; higher-value benefits typically require 50% or 100% ratings. Contact the Vermont veterans agency for current eligibility thresholds.

benefit_name: WSHFC Veterans Downpayment Assistance Loan Program
summary: The Washington State Housing Finance Commission (WSHFC) offers veterans a second-mortgage down payment assistance loan of up to $10,000 at 3% interest over a 10-year term. Available for use with an eligible first mortgage. Applicants must complete an approved homebuyer education course prior to closing.
eligibility: Honorably discharged Washington veterans, including National Guard and Reserve veterans. Surviving spouses of qualifying veterans also eligible. Must be a first-time homebuyer or purchasing in a targeted area. Income limits apply. Call 206-464-7139 or visit wshfc.org for current limits and participating lenders.
